title: Friday Sermon 6 September 2002
description:
Those who believe in Allah and His Messenger are the Truthful, and the Witnesses in the sight of their Lord, they will have their reward and their light.  But those who disregard and reject our Signs are the inmates of Hell.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Hadid [Iron]: 20).

A countryman came to the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) and asked in relation to this that who are those who are neither Prophets nor martyrs and yet the Prophets and martyrs are jealous at their standing and proximity before Almighty God? The Holy Prophet replied that they belong to simplistic and un-renowned families.  They are not even related to one another but that they loved one another for the sake of Allah and were sincere.  Allah the Almighty will set up altars of light for the on the Day of Judgment and station them upon them.  Then He would illumine their faces and their fill their clothes with light:

(Ahmad ibn Hanbal.  'Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal').

Allah the Almighty would not let the efforts of those who nullify themselves in the path of Allah the Almighty and walk in this path with complete steadfastness and endeavour  go to waste and He would grant them light and luminosity.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  March 1901.  'Malfuzat: ii'.  109).

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020906-UR.pdf
